groups.title: Essentials
groups.description: Sender identity, audience, objective, message facts, CTA destination, language, and recipient-consent confirmation.

groups.title: Audience & Personalization
groups.description: Recipient relationship and safe personalization controls for WhatsApp broadcast wording.

groups.title: Message Style & Timing
groups.description: Supporting facts, tone, length, emoji use, and time-sensitive details.

groups.title: Compliance & Output
groups.description: Offer terms, opt-out wording, sensitive category, output quantity, and final requirements.

variables.label: Sender or business name
variables.hint: Name recipients should immediately recognize as the sender.

help.what: Provide the sender or business name for this WhatsApp broadcast.
help.why: WhatsApp broadcasts should clearly identify the person, organization, or business contacting the recipient.
help.avoid: Avoid vague placeholders, unsupported claims, multiple competing topics, or changing the broadcast into a different campaign.

variables.label: Broadcast type
variables.hint: Choose the situation that best matches this broadcast.

help.what: Optional context for the broadcast type field.
help.why: Different WhatsApp broadcasts require different structures, levels of urgency, and information.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake scarcity, hidden promotion, fabricated personalization, extra CTAs, or sensitive private data.

variables.label: What is this about?
variables.hint: Name the announcement, offer, event, update, or topic.

help.what: Provide the what is this about? for this WhatsApp broadcast.
help.why: It establishes the central subject around which the entire message is written.
help.example: Good example: New booking slots for August
help.avoid: Avoid vague placeholders, unsupported claims, multiple competing topics, or changing the broadcast into a different campaign.

variables.label: Primary goal
variables.hint: Choose the single most important action or outcome.

help.what: Provide the primary goal for this WhatsApp broadcast.
help.why: The goal determines the message emphasis and primary CTA.
help.example: Good example: Drive purchase or booking
help.avoid: Avoid vague placeholders, unsupported claims, multiple competing topics, or changing the broadcast into a different campaign.

variables.label: Target audience
variables.placeholder: Existing customers who booked a service in the past six months
variables.hint: Describe who will receive the broadcast and what matters to them.

help.what: Provide the target audience for this WhatsApp broadcast.
help.why: Audience context materially affects vocabulary, relevance, assumed knowledge, and positioning.
help.example: Good example: Existing customers who booked a service in the past six months.
help.avoid: Avoid vague placeholders, unsupported claims, multiple competing topics, or changing the broadcast into a different campaign.

variables.label: Key message and facts
variables.placeholder: Explain the main news, value, action, and confirmed details recipients need.
variables.hint: Include only information that is accurate and approved for use.

help.what: Provide the key message and facts for this WhatsApp broadcast.
help.why: The model must receive the factual substance of the broadcast rather than inventing it.
help.example: Good example: August booking slots are now open. Weekend appointments are limited and can be reserved through the booking page.
help.avoid: Avoid assumptions, fake urgency, invented prices, fabricated results, unsupported links, or vague filler.

variables.label: Call to action
variables.placeholder: Reply BOOK, reserve your place, or view the details
variables.hint: Leave as Auto to generate one action that matches the goal.

help.what: Optional context for the call to action field.
help.why: It lets the user specify an exact action while retaining a low-effort automatic option.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake scarcity, hidden promotion, fabricated personalization, extra CTAs, or sensitive private data.

variables.label: Destination link
variables.hint: Add the exact page recipients should open, when relevant.

help.what: Optional context for the destination link field.
help.why: The model must never fabricate a booking, product, event, or information link.
help.avoid: Avoid invented, shortened, rewritten, tracking-modified, or unsupported links.

variables.label: Output language
variables.hint: Enter any language or regional language variant.

help.what: Optional context for the output language field.
help.why: It supports any required language without an oversized language selector.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake scarcity, hidden promotion, fabricated personalization, extra CTAs, or sensitive private data.

variables.label: Recipient consent confirmed
variables.hint: Confirm this audience has valid permission to receive the WhatsApp communication.

help.what: Provide the recipient consent confirmed for this WhatsApp broadcast.
help.why: The template should not generate unsolicited broadcast marketing without a positive consent confirmation.
help.avoid: Avoid generating messages for audiences without explicit WhatsApp permission or unclear consent status.

variables.label: Audience relationship
variables.hint: Clarifies what relationship may be referenced without inventing familiarity.

help.what: Optional context for the audience relationship field.
help.why: The wording should reflect the actual recipient relationship without pretending a closer connection exists.
help.example: Good example: Existing customers or subscribers
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake scarcity, hidden promotion, fabricated personalization, extra CTAs, or sensitive private data.

variables.label: Personalization
variables.hint: Use only supplied tokens or broad segment context.

help.what: Optional context for the personalization field.
help.why: It controls useful personalization while preventing fabricated individual details.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake scarcity, hidden promotion, fabricated personalization, extra CTAs, or sensitive private data.

variables.label: First-name token
variables.hint: Enter the exact merge token supported by your sending system.

help.what: Optional context for the first-name token field.
help.why: Different WhatsApp tools use different merge-token formats that cannot be safely inferred.
help.avoid: Avoid fake merge fields, unsupported tokens, or personalization based on private behavior that was not supplied.

variables.label: Supporting details
variables.placeholder: Add confirmed prices, locations, instructions, availability, or practical details.
variables.hint: Include useful secondary information that does not belong in the main message field.

help.what: Optional context for the supporting details field.
help.why: It accommodates important facts without expanding the required Essentials form.
help.example: Good example: Appointments are available from August 5. Deposits are refundable until 48 hours before the booking.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake scarcity, hidden promotion, fabricated personalization, extra CTAs, or sensitive private data.

variables.label: Tone
variables.hint: Choose how the sender should sound.

help.what: Optional context for the tone field.
help.why: Tone materially affects direct-message trust and brand consistency.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake scarcity, hidden promotion, fabricated personalization, extra CTAs, or sensitive private data.

variables.label: Message length
variables.hint: Controls detail while keeping the broadcast mobile-friendly.

help.what: Optional context for the message length field.
help.why: Different announcements require different amounts of explanation.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake scarcity, hidden promotion, fabricated personalization, extra CTAs, or sensitive private data.

variables.label: Emoji use
variables.hint: Controls whether emojis are omitted or used sparingly.

help.what: Optional context for the emoji use field.
help.why: Emoji expectations vary significantly between brands and audience types.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake scarcity, hidden promotion, fabricated personalization, extra CTAs, or sensitive private data.

variables.label: Date or deadline
variables.hint: Include the date, time, timezone, deadline, or affected period.

help.what: Optional context for the date or deadline field.
help.why: Time-sensitive information must come from the user and must not be inferred or fabricated.
help.avoid: Avoid invented deadlines, vague relative timing, missing time zones, or fake urgency.

variables.label: Offer terms
variables.placeholder: Eligibility, exclusions, valid dates, stock limits, minimum spend, or other terms
variables.hint: Provide the conditions needed to describe the offer accurately.

help.what: Optional context for the offer terms field.
help.why: Promotional wording can become misleading when important limitations are omitted.
help.example: Good example: Valid for opted-in customers only; subject to available appointment times.
help.avoid: Avoid invented discounts, stock limits, eligibility, expiry dates, bonuses, prices, or scarcity.

variables.label: Opt-out wording
variables.hint: Auto includes opt-out wording when the message context calls for it.

help.what: Optional context for the opt-out wording field.
help.why: Promotional and recurring broadcasts may need a clear, practical way to stop future messages.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake scarcity, hidden promotion, fabricated personalization, extra CTAs, or sensitive private data.

variables.label: Custom opt-out text
variables.placeholder: Reply STOP to stop receiving these updates.
variables.hint: Enter approved wording supported by your unsubscribe process.

help.what: Optional context for the custom opt-out text field.
help.why: The opt-out instruction must match the sender's actual process.
help.example: Good example: Reply STOP to stop receiving these updates.
help.avoid: Avoid unsubscribe instructions that the sender's real process cannot honor.

variables.label: Sensitive category
variables.hint: Applies additional caution to regulated or high-stakes claims.

help.what: Optional context for the sensitive category field.
help.why: Certain subject areas require more cautious wording and stronger claim controls.
help.avoid: Avoid unsupported medical, financial, legal, employment, political, or regulated-product claims.

variables.label: Number of variants
variables.hint: Generate one focused message or a small set of distinct options.

help.what: Optional context for the number of variants field.
help.why: It provides useful choice without producing an excessive number of near-duplicate messages.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake scarcity, hidden promotion, fabricated personalization, extra CTAs, or sensitive private data.

variables.label: Additional requirements
variables.placeholder: Add required wording, restrictions, brand preferences, or details to avoid.
variables.hint: Use this for important instructions not covered elsewhere.

help.what: Optional context for the additional requirements field.
help.why: It provides one controlled place for exceptional requirements without duplicating miscellaneous fields.
help.example: Good example: Avoid fake scarcity and do not mention discounts unless supplied.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake scarcity, hidden promotion, fabricated personalization, extra CTAs, or sensitive private data.
